name: Curie Component Manifest
description: Machine-readable catalog of Curie UI components. Each entry carries identity (what it is), usage judgment (when to reach for it), anatomy, and a canonical HTML snippet extracted verbatim from the golden pages. Styling lives in components.css / layout.css — snippets carry no inline paint. This file drives the docs site build AND is the primary consumption surface for AI agents/plugins.

components.name: Side Nav
components.purpose: The fixed left sidebar that provides primary navigation across the Curie app's top-level sections (Home, Briefs, Podcasts, Editorial, Games, Newsletter) with the Curie logo at top.
components.whenToUse: On every full app page as the persistent left-hand navigation chrome. Mark the current section's link with the `active` class.
components.whenNotToUse: Not for in-page or sub-section navigation, and not on standalone/embedded views that have no app shell.
anatomy: side-nav__link (a; add .active for current page) with icon (img or inline svg) + side-nav__label
components.interactions: Each side-nav__link navigates to its section page; the current page's link carries the `active` modifier for highlight. Hover states are handled in CSS.

Curie

  • Editorial
  • Games
  • Newsletter

components.name: Top Banner
components.purpose: A full-width gradient promotional banner pinned above the app content, pairing a headline/subtext with a pill call-to-action button (used to promote the Weekly Dose newsletter subscription).
components.whenToUse: At the very top of a page (before the sidebar/main) when you need a single prominent, dismissible-feeling marketing or subscription prompt.
components.whenNotToUse: For per-item inline messaging, error/status alerts, or anywhere more than one competing CTA is needed.
anatomy: top-banner__left > top-banner__title-group (icon-inner svg + headline) and top-banner__sub-group (sub text)
components.interactions: The top-banner__btn is the single actionable element (subscribe CTA); the rest is static content.
components.snippet:
Prefer the summary version?
Stay updated in minutes with Curie's Weekly Dose

components.name: Page Header
components.purpose: The standard page title row for app sections: a section icon and title on the left, and the signed-in user's avatar and name on the right.
components.whenToUse: At the top of a section's main content area (Briefs, Games, Podcasts, etc.) to label the page and show the current user.
components.whenNotToUse: On the Editorial section, which uses the dedicated editorial-header variant instead.
components.interactions: Static header; the user-avatar/user-name identify the current user and are non-interactive in the golden reference.

components.name: Editorial Header
components.purpose: The Editorial section's title row — a media icon plus the 'Curie Editorial' title on the left and the user chip on the right. A styling variant of the page header specific to Editorial.
components.whenToUse: At the top of the Editorial section pages (editorial and editorial-detail).
components.whenNotToUse: On non-Editorial sections; use the generic page-header there.
anatomy: editorial-header__left (optional editorial-header__back circle + editorial-header__icon img + editorial-header__title h1)
components.interactions: Static header; identifies the section and current user.

components.name: Footer
components.purpose: The page footer holding the copyright line, social media links (LinkedIn, X, Facebook), and legal links (Privacy Policy and Terms & Conditions).
components.whenToUse: At the very bottom of a page's main content column to close out the page with legal and social links.
components.whenNotToUse: For in-content link groups or navigation; this is the terminal page chrome only.
anatomy: footer__link (Privacy Policy and Terms & Conditions anchors, separated by a middot)
components.interactions: footer__social-link anchors open social profiles; the footer__link anchors open the Privacy Policy and the Terms & Conditions. All are links.

components.name: Editorial Card
components.purpose: Entry point to a long-form editorial piece. Thumbnail-led, title + teaser + author row.
components.whenToUse: Grids of long-form written content (editorials, features). Use in 3-column .editorial-grid layouts.
components.whenNotToUse: Short scannable items (use brief-item) or audio content (use pod-card).
components.interactions: Whole card is an anchor; carries hover elevation + read-more arrow affordance from components.css.
components.snippet:
Rising Voices in Oncology

A curated look at the oncologists shaping the next era of cancer care — through research...

By Curie Team Read more

components.name: Brief Item
components.purpose: Compact, scannable row/tile for a clinical brief: source icon, date, truncated title, source name.
components.whenToUse: Dense lists and horizontal scroll rails of briefs where an HCP scans many items fast.
components.whenNotToUse: Featured or hero placements (use the featured brief treatment on the Briefs page).
components.interactions: Hover raises surface to --color-bg-card-hover (from components.css).
components.snippet:
Apr 15, 2026
Two surgical approaches for bone...
Ortiz.inc

components.name: Podcast Card
components.purpose: Audio episode tile: thumbnail with play affordance, date, title.
components.whenToUse: Horizontal-scroll podcast rails and podcast grids.
components.whenNotToUse: Written content of any kind.
components.interactions: Card opens the audio player; play glyph overlays the thumbnail.
components.snippet:
Jan 13, 2026
What Tennis Teaches Doctors About Medicine

components.name: Game Card
components.purpose: Tile for a Curie game: title, illustration, description, play action.
components.whenToUse: Games hub grid and game previews on Home.
components.whenNotToUse: Anything that is not an interactive game.
components.interactions: Play button is the single action; card carries standard hover elevation.
components.snippet:
Curie Connections

Find the hidden connections among this mix of medical terms.

Curie Pinpoint

Use clues to pinpoint the medical theme that ties them all together.

Curie Strands

Connect the dots between medical concepts in this word-linking challenge.

components.name: Profile Card
components.purpose: Presents an author or featured expert with avatar, name, institution, social links, topic tags, and a short bio.
components.whenToUse: On editorial detail pages to introduce contributing authors or featured experts, or anywhere a person needs a compact profile summary.
components.whenNotToUse: For linking to a story or article (use a story/related-story card) or for non-person entities.
components.interactions: Social links are anchors; the card itself is static (no hover state on the container).
components.snippet:
Marla Lipsyc-Sharf
UCLA Health
Breast Oncology

One of the few voices actively focused on breast cancer in young women — her research on fertility, treatment timing, pregnancy after diagnosis, and circulating tumor DNA monitoring is filling a long-neglected gap in the field.

components.name: Related Story Card
components.purpose: A compact clickable tile linking to a related article, showing a thumbnail image, title, and source/time metadata.
components.whenToUse: In a 'Related stories' grid on brief or editorial detail pages to surface recommended reads.
components.whenNotToUse: As the primary list item for a full briefs feed (use latest-card) or to profile a person (use profile-card).
components.interactions: Entire card is an anchor link to the related story.
components.snippet:
Many heart disease patients have blood pressure below...

Medscapeabout 17 hours ago

components.name: Latest Card
components.purpose: The primary feed row for the Latest briefs list: a full-width card with title, meta, description, dive-in link, thumbnail, and an actions bar (read, bookmark, vote, share).
components.whenToUse: For each item in the 'Latest' briefs feed on the briefs list page.
components.whenNotToUse: For compact related-reads tiles (use related-story-card) or hero imagery on a detail page (use brief-detail-image).
components.interactions: The card has an onclick navigating to the brief detail; the 'Dive in' link and the actions bar (read check, bookmark, up/down vote, share) are individually interactive.
components.snippet:
Remote heart monitoring device slashes hospital readmissions by 69%

about 17 hours ago · Medscape

A wireless heart device that monitors pressure in the lungs' blood vessels is proving to be a game-changer for heart failure patients. The CardioMEMS system helped slash hospital readmissions by 69% in a major study of 304 patients across Europe and Australia. The implantable sensor lets doctors adjust medications remotely based on daily pressure readings, keeping patients out of the hospital while improving their quality of life.

Dive in →

components.name: Brief Card
components.purpose: The personalized 'For You' briefs-feed card: a thumbnail-led clinical brief with title, relative-date + source-count meta, and a compact actions row (read, bookmark, up/down vote, share). Comes in four widths for the mixed feed grid.
components.whenToUse: For each item in the personalized 'For You' briefs feed, across the mixed-width grid — a featured hero card, a wide row, compact stacked cards, and 3-up vertical grid tiles.
components.whenNotToUse: For the single-column 'Latest' feed (use latest-card, which shows a named source, description, Dive-in link and share/read actions) or compact related-reads tiles (use related-story-card).
anatomy: card (container; width modifier: feat-main | wcard | hcard | gcard)
card-img (thumbnail; wrapped in img-wrap for the wide variant)
actions (act read, act bmark, votes with vbtn up/dn, act share)
components.interactions: The card navigates to the brief detail (onclick on the featured variant). The actions row — mark-read, bookmark, up/down vote, share — are individually interactive. Hover raises the surface (components.css).
components.snippet:
Lower cholesterol targets cut heart events by one-third in landmark study

about 17 hours ago8 Sources

Targeting LDL cholesterol below 55 mg/dL instead of 70 mg/dL reduced major cardiovascular events by 33% in patients with heart disease, according to the Ez-PAVE trial. The South Korean study of over 3,000 patients used mostly statins and ezetimibe to achieve these aggressive targets.

components.name: Brief Detail Hero Image
components.purpose: The hero image block at the top of a brief detail page, wrapping a full-width lead image.
components.whenToUse: As the lead visual on a brief detail page, directly above the title.
components.whenNotToUse: For thumbnails inside feed or related cards (use their built-in image elements).
components.interactions: Static, non-interactive.

components.name: Filter Dropdown
components.purpose: Label + current value + chevron trigger with an attached selection menu, for narrowing dense lists.
components.whenToUse: List/browse pages where an HCP narrows by specialty, indication, or resource type.
components.whenNotToUse: Single on/off toggles or navigation.
components.interactions: filter-btn toggles filter-menu; .active marks the current selection. Menu open state is class-driven.
components.snippet: Specialty:Cardiology
components.previewNote: Menu forced visible for documentation; in product it opens on trigger click.

states.label: Closed
states.snippet: Specialty:Cardiology

states.label: Open

components.name: Segmented Toggle
components.purpose: A two-option segmented control with an animated sliding indicator for switching between mutually exclusive views within a list.
components.whenToUse: Use to switch between a small fixed set (2-3) of peer views of the same content stream, such as 'For You' vs 'Latest' on a feed.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for more than 3 options, for navigation between pages, or for multi-select filtering (use the filter dropdowns instead).
anatomy: seg-toggle__btn (option button; .active marks the current selection)
components.interactions: Clicking a button activates it and slides the __slider under it; JS toggles the .active class and translates the slider. Exactly one button is active at a time.

states.name: first
states.label: First active

states.name: second
states.label: Second active

components.name: Form Field
components.purpose: Labelled text input with required marker — the atomic unit of Curie forms.
components.whenToUse: Any capture flow (newsletter signup, preferences).
components.whenNotToUse: Filtering or search over existing content (use filter / search patterns).
anatomy: form-input-wrap + form-input__icon (optional leading icon; input gets form-input--icon)
is-error modifier (rose fill + border on input/select)
form-error (inline validation row — see the form-error component)
components.interactions: Focus ring from components.css. Add .is-error to the input/select for the error state (rose fill + border) and pair it with a form-error row. A form-input-wrap with a form-input__icon adds a leading icon (input gets form-input--icon to reserve space); form-help shows supporting text below the field.

components.name: Error Input Field
components.purpose: A labelled form input in its invalid state — rose fill and border on the control, paired with an inline error message that names the fix.
components.whenToUse: Whenever a field fails validation and the user must correct it before proceeding (malformed NPI or email, an unselected required option).
components.whenNotToUse: For neutral guidance (use form-help) or page-level / transient feedback (use toast). For the message row on its own, see form-error.
anatomy: form-error (inline validation row: alert icon + message)
components.interactions: Add .is-error to the form-input or form-select to switch it to the rose error surface (--color-error-surface) with a --color-error border, then render a form-error row directly beneath. Remove .is-error and the form-error row once the field becomes valid. Same building blocks as the form-field Error variant, documented here as a standalone state.
components.snippet: Enter a valid 10-digit NPI number

variants.label: Text input error
variants.snippet: Enter a valid 10-digit NPI number

variants.label: Select error
variants.snippet: Please select your specialty

components.name: Inline Newsletter Bar
components.purpose: A compact horizontal newsletter-subscription prompt with icon, title, subtitle and a single subscribe button, designed to sit inline within a content list.
components.whenToUse: Use to surface a low-friction newsletter signup inline between content cards without interrupting the reading flow.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use as the primary signup destination or when collecting more than an implicit opt-in (use the full signup-card for name/email/specialty capture).
components.interactions: Clicking nl-bar__btn initiates the free subscription flow. The bar is a static inline promo with a single hover-able button.
components.snippet:
Prefer the summary version?
Stay updated in minutes with Curie's Weekly Dose.

components.name: Newsletter Signup Card
components.purpose: A self-contained newsletter signup form card capturing name, email and specialty, with a prominent subscribe button.
components.whenToUse: Use as the primary conversion surface on a dedicated newsletter or landing page where you collect HCP details for subscription.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use inline within a busy feed (use nl-bar) or when only an email opt-in is needed.
components.interactions: User fills name, email and selects a specialty; required fields are marked with .req asterisks. Clicking btn-subscribe submits the subscription.
components.snippet:
Get Curie's Weekly Dose

Free. Weekly. Quick to read.

components.name: Button
components.purpose: The systematic action control, grounded in the golden set. Pill-shaped label buttons in four emphasis levels — primary (the solid purple button), secondary, border, text — plus a full-width primary preset (Subscribe) and a round icon-button family in three skins (ghost, solid, glass) and three sizes. Every type carries a full set of interaction states. Includes gradient CTA skins for the high-emphasis feed / banner actions.
components.whenToUse: Any triggered action. For a labelled action pick the type by emphasis: one primary as the loud gesture of a view, secondary for its neutral peer, border for a lower-emphasis alternative, text for the quietest inline action. For an icon-only action (play, next, bookmark, more) use btn--round: solid (btn--primary) for the main play/pause gesture, glass for media/carousel nav over imagery, ghost for meta actions on a card.
components.whenNotToUse: Navigation between pages (use links / nav) or one-off product-specific CTAs that already have a dedicated component (btn-subscribe, home-footer__button, see-more-button).
anatomy: btn (base: pill sizing, type, focus ring, transitions — matches the golden hero/top-banner pill)
btn--primary | btn--secondary | btn--border | btn--text (label emphasis type — exactly one)
btn--round (circular icon button) + skin: default = ghost, btn--primary = solid purple, btn--glass = frosted
icon + label: place an inline <svg> before (leading, e.g. Subscribe to Curie) or after (trailing, e.g. Read Article →) the text inside any .btn type — the base auto-sizes it to 18px with gap
btn-subscribe (full-width primary preset for form submits — btn btn--primary btn-subscribe)
btn--gradient (indigo→cyan feed CTA) · btn--gradient-accent (purple→teal) · btn--gradient-brief/--pod/--game (home-banner theme skins) — high-emphasis gradient CTAs
disabled attribute for the disabled state
is-hover | is-focus | is-active | is-disabled (helpers that pin a visual state for specimens or JS-driven controls)
votes + vbtn.up / vbtn.dn (segmented vote pill; hover shows sentiment — green up / red down)
meta-actions + ma-btn (ghost circular action button) + ma-votes/ma-vbtn (the brief content action bar)
components.interactions: Label buttons: :hover (lift + brighter fill), :focus-visible (purple focus ring), :active (darken + press-scale), :disabled (40% opacity, no pointer events). Round buttons scale on :hover (1.08, glass 1.1) and press in on :active, sharing the same focus ring and disabled behaviour. The matching is-* classes force each visual state for specimens or from script.
components.snippet:
<button class="btn btn--primary">Primary</button>
<button class="btn btn--secondary">Secondary</button>
<button class="btn btn--border">Border</button>
<button class="btn btn--text">Text</button>

<!-- Full-width primary preset, e.g. a form submit -->
<button class="btn btn--primary btn-subscribe">Subscribe Free</button>

<button class="btn btn--round btn--primary" aria-label="Play"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor"><path d="M8 5v14l11-7z"/></svg></button>
<button class="btn btn--round" aria-label="Bookmark"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M19 21l-7-5-7 5V5a2 2 0 012-2h10a2 2 0 012 2z"/></svg></button>
<button class="btn btn--round btn--glass" aria-label="Next"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="9,6 15,12 9,18"/></svg></button>

components.name: Content Badge
components.purpose: Type + date eyebrow identifying what a piece of content is before the reader commits.
components.whenToUse: On hero/banner content and featured cards where content type isn't obvious from context.
components.whenNotToUse: Inside dense lists where the container already declares the type.
components.interactions: None — purely informational.
components.snippet: PODCAST Dec 2, 2025

components.name: User Avatar
components.purpose: A compact circular initials chip identifying the signed-in user, optionally paired with the user's full name.
components.whenToUse: Inside a page or editorial header's right slot to represent the current user; use with the adjacent user-name span when space allows.
components.whenNotToUse: For decorative avatars, lists of many users, or where a real profile photo is required — this is an initials-only chip.
components.interactions: Static identity indicator; non-interactive in the golden reference.
components.snippet:
mr
moumitra roychowdhury

components.name: Editorial Hero
components.purpose: A full-width editorial hero block with a photographic background, dark left-to-right gradient overlay, and left-aligned content: tags, accented title, subtitle, primary/secondary actions, and reading metadata.
components.whenToUse: Use at the top of an editorial feature or detail page to establish topic, set tone, and offer the primary subscribe/share actions.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use on list or home pages (use home-banner), or where no photographic background/editorial framing is appropriate.
components.interactions: Static hero; the two hero__btn actions (Subscribe, Share) are clickable CTAs. Background and overlay are decorative CSS layers.
components.snippet:
Curie Editorial March 2026

Rising Voices inOncology

A curated look at the oncologists shaping the next era of cancer care — through research, advocacy, and digital influence.

5 min read
10 Voices Featured

components.name: Home Hero Banner Carousel
components.purpose: The home page hero carousel that rotates through themed content slides (podcast, brief, game), each with a photographic background, gradient overlays, content-type badge, title, description and a themed primary action, plus thumbnail navigation.
components.whenToUse: Use at the top of the home page to spotlight rotating featured content across content types, with the active theme driving the banner's accent color.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use on detail or list pages (use the editorial hero), or when only a single static feature needs highlighting.
anatomy: home-banner (carousel container; theme-active class drives accent)
banner-slide (one per slide; theme class + .active on current, data-slide index)
components.interactions: JS rotates slides by toggling .active on banner-slide and swapping the home-banner theme class to recolor accents; nav-arrow and thumbnail-item clicks change the active slide. Snippet trimmed to one representative (podcast) slide.
components.snippet:
PODCAST Dec 2, 2025

What an Active Shooter Taught an Oncologist

When an active-shooter alert swept across her hospital campus, oncologist and educator Dr. Beatrice Preti found herself experiencing a level of fear she'd never encountered in medicine before, even after years of caring for patients with life-limiting diagnoses.

BRIEFApr 13, 2026

Two surgical approaches for bone metastases show timing-based advantages

A new meta-analysis comparing surgical options for femoral bone metastases found that both intramedullary nailing and endoprosthetic reconstruction are effective, but with different timing advantages. IM nailing showed fewer early complications within 6 months, while...

GAMEApr 13, 2026

Daily Connections Puzzle

Exercise your brain with this addictive word association game. Find the connecting threads between seemingly unrelated words. From obvious groupings to cleverly disguised categories, each puzzle will challenge your lateral thinking.

components.name: Almanac Meeting Card
components.purpose: A wide list-row card that triages a medical conference for HCPs — event date, name, specialty domain, an editorial priority tier (must-track / field-wide / niche), an Editor's Take blurb, and Track / View-details actions.
components.whenToUse: In a 'Conferences to track' / almanac list where an HCP scans upcoming congresses and decides which are worth their time. One row per meeting.
components.whenNotToUse: For a podcast recap of a conference (use conf-card / pod-card) or any non-event content.
anatomy: almanac-card__take (almanac-card__take-header with EDITOR'S TAKE label + almanac-card__take-body)
components.interactions: Track bookmarks the meeting; View details opens the meeting page. Tier badge and Editor's Take are informational. Purple palette; the Editor's Take accent rail is teal in every tier.

components.name: AI Tool Card
components.purpose: A launcher tile for an AI-powered clinical utility — icon, title, description, optional capability tags, a speed/provenance meta line, and a full-width primary CTA.
components.whenToUse: In a 'Curie Tools' grid where HCPs pick a productivity tool (handout generation, prior-auth drafting, drug lookup). One card per tool.
components.whenNotToUse: For content (briefs, editorials, podcasts) — use the relevant content card. Tags are optional and may be omitted (e.g. the drug-lookup variant).
components.interactions: The CTA button launches the tool. Card lifts on hover. Purple palette across every variant.
components.snippet:
Patient Handout Generator

Generate a patient-ready handout for any regimen in seconds — written in plain language and sourced from approved labeling.

Regimen-specificFDA-sourcedFor oncologists
<60 seconds · Built from FDA-approved labeling

components.name: Game Mode Card
components.purpose: A dense game-mode tile for a specific learning game — a color-coded mode badge, title, description, a progress bar, a stats row (time, learning points, credits) and a prominent Play Now CTA.
components.whenToUse: On the Games page, in a 'More ways to play' grid, to present the different modes of a game (matching, myth-or-bust, confidence) with progress and reward context.
components.whenNotToUse: For the illustration-led game launcher (use game-card) or the compact preview tile (use game-tile).
components.interactions: Play Now starts the mode. The progress bar reflects completion. Badge accent is teal / blue / pink per mode; the Play button is purple in every mode.
components.snippet:
MATCHING

Primary Care Match the Pairs

Match each clinical sign to the condition it most points to, against the clock.

5 min20 LP0.25 Cr

components.name: Game Tile
components.purpose: A compact square game-preview tile — centered title, illustration, and a full-width Play button. A denser, description-less sibling of game-card for quick-play rails.
components.whenToUse: In a horizontal 'Quick play' rail (Games page or a Home game rail) where space is tight and the game is already familiar.
components.whenNotToUse: For the full games grid with descriptions (use game-card) or a game with modes/progress (use game-mode-card).
components.interactions: Play starts the game; the tile scales slightly on hover. Purple-translucent surface.

components.name: Coming Soon Note
components.purpose: A full-width dashed-border notice bar that closes out a list, signalling more content is on the way.
components.whenToUse: At the end of a grid or feed (e.g. below the games grid) to set the expectation that the list will grow.
components.whenNotToUse: For actionable empty states that need a CTA, or per-item status (use a badge).
components.interactions: Static, non-interactive.
components.snippet: More games coming soon — check back for new challenges!

components.name: Multi-select Chips
components.purpose: A pick-many form control that shows selected values as removable chips inside an input-styled field with a chevron.
components.whenToUse: When an HCP selects several values from a set (therapeutic areas, specialties) and needs to see and remove each selection.
components.whenNotToUse: For a single choice (use form-select) or on/off state (use toggle).
components.interactions: Clicking the field opens the option menu (product); each chip's × removes that value. Chips use the purple primary fill.
components.snippet: Oncology Neurology

components.name: Toggle Switch
components.purpose: A single on/off switch for a boolean preference, with an adjacent label.
components.whenToUse: For opt-in / opt-out preferences (notifications, digests). Add .is-on for the on state, .is-disabled for a locked control.
components.whenNotToUse: For choosing among options (use radio-group) or multiple selections (use multiselect).
components.interactions: Clicking the track flips it and slides the knob; the on state fills the track purple. Disabled dims the control and label.
components.snippet: Daily briefing notifications

variants.label: On
variants.snippet: Daily briefing notifications

variants.label: Off
variants.snippet: Weekly digest

variants.label: Disabled
variants.snippet: Marketing emails

components.name: Radio Group
components.purpose: A pick-one control for a small set of options, each optionally carrying a supporting description.
components.whenToUse: For mutually-exclusive choices (newsletter frequency, role). Mark the chosen option's row with .is-selected.
components.whenNotToUse: For many/searchable options (use form-select) or multi-select (use multiselect).
anatomy: radio (option row; .is-selected on the chosen one)
components.interactions: Selecting a row moves the filled ring to it (purple accent). Can be paired with a form-error row for group validation.

states.name: selected
states.label: Selected

states.name: unselected
states.label: Unselected

components.name: Field Error Message
components.purpose: An inline validation row — a rose alert icon plus a short message — shown beneath an errored field.
components.whenToUse: Directly under any form control in its error state (input, select, multiselect, radio, toggle) to explain what needs fixing. Pair with .is-error on the control.
components.whenNotToUse: For page-level or transient feedback (use toast) or neutral help text (use form-help).
components.interactions: Static; appears/disappears with the field's validity. Uses --color-error.
components.snippet: Enter a valid 10-digit NPI number

components.name: Toast Notification
components.purpose: A transient, dismissible status message in four semantic variants — success, error, warning, info — with an accent icon disc, title/message, and a close affordance.
components.whenToUse: For feedback on an action or a system event (subscribe confirmed, save failed, offline, new content). Stack in a toast-stack.
components.whenNotToUse: For field-level validation (use form-error) or persistent page content.
anatomy: toast__disc (semantic icon disc; warning uses a bare toast__icon-warning instead)
components.interactions: Auto-dismisses or is closed via toast__close. The disc/icon color and glyph encode the semantic: teal success, rose error, amber warning, blue info.

components.name: Modal
components.purpose: A centered dialog card layered over a dimmed overlay to focus the user on a single decision or task.
components.whenToUse: Use to interrupt the flow for a short, self-contained action such as a confirmation, form, or focused message.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-blocking or transient notices, or for content large enough to warrant its own page.
components.interactions: The overlay opens with .is-open; the close button lifts and rings on hover and focus, and footer actions dismiss or confirm.
components.snippet:
Archive this brief?

Archived briefs move to your library and stop appearing in the daily feed. You can restore them at any time.

components.name: Accordion
components.purpose: A vertical stack of expandable items that disclose their panel content one section at a time.
components.whenToUse: Use to compress long, scannable content such as FAQs or grouped details into collapsible sections.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use when all content must be visible at once or when there is only a single short passage.
components.interactions: Clicking a trigger toggles .is-open on its item, rotating the chevron and revealing the panel; items lift on hover.
components.snippet:
Each brief distills the most relevant studies, approvals, and commentary for your specialty into a five-minute read.

Briefs refresh every morning before 7am in your local time zone.

components.name: Tooltip
components.purpose: A small floating bubble that reveals a short label or hint for the element it wraps.
components.whenToUse: Use to clarify an icon-only control or add a brief supplemental note on hover or focus.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for essential content, long text, or anything that must remain visible or be interacted with.
components.interactions: The bubble fades and slides in on hover, keyboard focus-within, or a pinned .is-open, and points at the trigger via its arrow.

components.name: Dropdown Menu
components.purpose: A trigger that opens a floating list of actions or selectable options anchored beneath it.
components.whenToUse: Use to group related commands or a filter's choices behind a single compact control.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for primary navigation between pages or for a long option set better served by a full page or search.
components.interactions: The trigger flips its chevron and toggles .is-open on the menu; items highlight on hover and mark the current choice with .is-active.

components.name: Tabs
components.purpose: A horizontal control that switches a single content region between several peer views.
components.whenToUse: Use to divide related content into a few named sections the user toggles between in place.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for sequential steps, for a single view, or when the number of sections would overflow the row.
components.interactions: Selecting a tab pins .is-active with a raised pill; inactive tabs brighten on hover and each tab shows a focus ring.
components.snippet: A high-level summary of the latest findings and why they matter to your practice.

components.name: Progress
components.purpose: A determinate horizontal bar that shows how far a task or value has advanced toward completion.
components.whenToUse: Use when the completion percentage is known, such as an upload, a reading position, or a multi-step form.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for indefinite waits with no measurable progress, or for a single boolean done/not-done state.
components.interactions: The fill animates its width from the inline percentage toward the target, carrying the teal-to-blue progress gradient.
components.snippet: Reading progress60%

components.name: Avatar
components.purpose: A compact circular representation of a person via their photo or initials fallback, alone or stacked in a group.
components.whenToUse: Use to identify an author, member, or participant, and to stack a group to imply a set of collaborators.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-person entities better shown with an icon, or where a full name label is required for clarity.
components.interactions: Avatars lift and glow on hover; grouped avatars overlap and raise the hovered one above its neighbours.

components.name: Badge
components.purpose: A small pill that labels or categorizes an item with a tinted status, plus a removable tag variant.
components.whenToUse: Use to tag status, type, or category inline, and use the tag variant for user-removable filters or keywords.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for interactive actions best served by a button, or for long phrases that would break the pill shape.
components.interactions: Tint variants are static labels; the tag variant adds a remove button that highlights and scales on hover, focus, and press.

components.name: Icon Button
components.purpose: The compact, icon-only actions that ride on every brief and story card — mark read, bookmark, upvote / downvote, and share. Circular stroked buttons (.act) plus a segmented vote pill (.votes / .vbtn), each with a sentiment-tinted hover. These are the exact controls used on the golden Briefs and Home cards.
components.whenToUse: For the per-card action row (read · bookmark · vote · share) and any compact glyph action in tight space. Reuse the same .act / .votes markup so every card reads as one system. Always give each control an accessible name (aria-label).
components.whenNotToUse: When the action needs a visible text label to be understood, or when it is the page's single loud primary CTA (use the full pill Button instead). Never ship it without an accessible name.
anatomy: actions (the flex row wrapping the card's action controls)
act (base circle, transparent with a 1px stroke; add read / bmark / share for the sentiment-tinted hover)
votes (segmented pill wrapping the two vote buttons)
vbtn up / vbtn dn (like / dislike segments; up tints green, dn tints red on hover)
inline svg glyph or brand-asset img (BookMark.svg, up arrow.svg, down arrow.svg)
components.interactions: Hover brightens the surface and lifts (scale 1.08); .act.read tints teal, .act.bmark tints gold, .act.share tints blue; .vbtn.up tints green and .vbtn.dn tints red (pinnable with .is-hover). Active presses in (scale 0.95).

variants.label: Read
Bookmark
Like
Dislike
Share

components.name: Checkbox
components.purpose: A single binary or tri-state control for opt-in choices and multi-select lists. It styles a real native checkbox, so keyboard, focus, and assistive-tech behaviour come for free while the box picks up the Curie purple fill.
components.whenToUse: For independent on/off choices, 'select all' rows (indeterminate), and any list where more than one option can be picked. Wrap the input and its text in a single <label class="checkbox"> so the whole row is clickable.
components.whenNotToUse: For mutually exclusive options (use radio) or an immediate settings switch (use toggle). Not for a primary action.
anatomy: checkbox__control (real input[type=checkbox], appearance:none, purple fill on :checked)
components.interactions: Hover brightens the border; :checked/.is-checked fills purple with a white check; .is-indeterminate shows a purple dash; :focus-visible/.is-focus shows a purple ring; :disabled/.is-disabled dims the control and mutes the label.

states.label: Indeterminate

states.label: Focus

components.name: Textarea
components.purpose: A multiline text input on the Curie form surface for longer free-text — notes, feedback, message bodies. It matches the single-line input surface and radius so forms read as one system.
components.whenToUse: When a response can run to multiple lines or paragraphs. Keep it vertically resizable so users can expand for longer answers.
components.whenNotToUse: For short single-line values (use a text/search input) or fixed-option choices (use select/checkbox/radio).
anatomy: textarea (the multiline control; add is-error for validation failure, disabled to lock)
components.interactions: Hover shows a faint border; :focus/.is-focus brightens the surface with a purple border + ring; .is-error paints the error surface and red border (and a red ring on focus); :disabled/.is-disabled dims and stops resizing.
components.snippet: <textarea class="textarea" placeholder="Share your notes on this brief…">Curie's summary tracked the KOL sentiment well — worth flagging the dosing caveat in the next digest.</textarea>

components.name: Search Input
components.purpose: A pill-shaped search field with a leading magnifier and an optional clear button, for filtering lists of briefs, podcasts, or editorial items.
components.whenToUse: Any time content can be filtered or looked up by keyword. Show the clear button once the field has a value so users can reset in one click.
components.whenNotToUse: For general free-text entry (use a plain input) or multi-line text (use textarea). Not a substitute for structured filters when facets are needed.
anatomy: search (pill wrapper, lights up on focus-within)
components.interactions: The whole wrapper brightens with a purple border + ring on focus-within (or pinned .is-focus). The clear button brightens on hover and resets the field. .is-disabled dims the wrapper and blocks input.

states.name: disabled
states.label: Disabled

components.name: Select
components.purpose: A styled native dropdown for choosing one value (or several with the multi variant) from a known list. It wraps a real <select>, so it keeps native keyboard and mobile picker behaviour under the Curie surface + chevron.
components.whenToUse: For a single choice from a moderate, known set of options (therapeutic area, sort order). Use .select--multi with a native multiple/size select when several values may be chosen.
components.whenNotToUse: For a handful of visible options where radios/toggles read better, for boolean settings, or for very large searchable datasets (use a combobox/typeahead instead).
anatomy: select (styled native <select> with token chevron; add is-error for validation, disabled to lock)
select--multi (variant: no chevron, scrolling list for <select multiple>)
components.interactions: Hover shows a faint border; :focus/.is-focus brightens the surface with a purple border + ring; .is-error paints the error surface + red border; :disabled/.is-disabled dims. In .select--multi a selected option is filled purple.
components.snippet:
<select class="select">
  <option>All therapeutic areas</option>
  <option>Oncology</option>
  <option>Immunology</option>
  <option>Cardiology</option>
</select>

components.name: Link
components.purpose: An inline text link in the brand purple, for navigating within prose or captions. It carries hover, focus, active, and visited states so it never reads as flat static text.
components.whenToUse: For inline navigation inside body copy, footnotes, and captions. Use the .lnk--external variant (trailing arrow) when the destination leaves the app or opens in a new tab.
components.whenNotToUse: For primary or standalone actions (use Button / Icon Button). Avoid stacking many links in one paragraph — restraint keeps the accent meaningful.
components.interactions: Hover shifts to the brighter accent and solidifies the underline; :active deepens to the primary purple; :visited mutes; :focus-visible/.is-focus shows a purple ring. Real states plus pinned .is-hover/.is-active/.is-focus helpers.
components.snippet: Curie summarized the latest oncology briefs this morning. Read the full study on PubMed for the underlying data.

components.name: Spinner
components.purpose: A rotating accent ring that signals an indeterminate wait — content loading, a request in flight, a background action processing.
components.whenToUse: Use for short, indeterminate waits where you cannot show progress, inside buttons, cards, or overlays while data resolves.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use when you can show real progress (use a progress bar), when the wait exceeds a few seconds for a whole view (use a skeleton), or as a decorative element.
components.interactions: Non-interactive. Spins continuously via a CSS keyframes rotation at a steady linear cadence until removed from the DOM.

variants.label: Small
Medium
Large

components.name: Skeleton
components.purpose: A shimmer placeholder that mirrors the shape of content still loading, reducing perceived wait and preventing layout shift.
components.whenToUse: Use while a view or card's content is fetching, laying skeletons in the same footprint the real content will occupy.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for very short waits (use a spinner), for a truly empty result (use an empty state), or as a permanent placeholder for missing data.
components.interactions: Non-interactive. A highlight band sweeps left-to-right on a looping shimmer keyframes animation until the real content replaces it.

variants.label: Text line
Circle (avatar)
Rect (media)

components.name: Empty State
components.purpose: A centered block that explains why a region has no content and offers a next action, turning a dead end into a guided moment.
components.whenToUse: Use when a list, search, or collection legitimately has nothing to show — first-run, cleared filters, or zero results — and you can point the user forward.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use while content is still loading (use a skeleton or spinner) or to communicate an error condition (use an inline alert).
components.interactions: The optional action is a composed .btn with its own hover/focus/active states; the container itself is static.

components.name: Inline Alert
components.purpose: An inline banner that surfaces a contextual status message — info, success, warning, or error — anchored next to the content it concerns.
components.whenToUse: Use to give persistent, in-context feedback tied to a section or form, using the semantic variant that matches the message's severity.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for transient, dismiss-on-timeout notifications (use a toast) or for empty regions (use an empty state).
components.interactions: Static banner; the optional .alert__close button has a hover state that brightens its color and dismisses the alert on click.

components.name: Divider
components.purpose: A hairline separator that groups content — a horizontal rule, a vertical inline rule, or a centered labeled break.
components.whenToUse: Use to separate stacked sections, split inline items (metadata rows, toolbars), or introduce a labeled break such as OR between choices.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use to box or frame content (use a card), and avoid stacking multiple dividers where whitespace alone would read cleaner.
components.interactions: Non-interactive presentational separator; no states.

components.name: Table
components.purpose: A readable dark data grid for tabular records — a header row of labelled columns over body rows separated by faint divider rules, floating on a card surface with a soft purple edge.
components.whenToUse: When content is genuinely tabular (rows of records sharing the same columns) and needs to be scanned or compared — leaderboards, metric breakdowns, roster or dosage tables. Use the zebra modifier when rows are dense and the eye needs help tracking across.
components.whenNotToUse: For a handful of key/value pairs (use a definition list), for card-like content that reads better as a grid of cards, or on narrow mobile widths where columns cannot breathe.
anatomy: table (the grid; add table--zebra for striped rows)
table__row (tr; add is-hover to pin the hover tint)
table__cell (td) — add table__cell--head for header cells, table__cell--right to right-align numerics
components.interactions: Body rows highlight on hover via table__row:hover (pinned with is-hover). Header cells are static labels. Alignment helpers do not change behaviour, only text alignment.

components.name: Pagination
components.purpose: A row of pill page controls for stepping through a long, paged list — numbered items flanked by prev/next arrows, with the current page filled in brand purple.
components.whenToUse: Below a paged list or table when the full set is split across pages and the user needs to jump between them. Mark the current page with is-active and dim unavailable arrows with is-disabled.
components.whenNotToUse: For continuously loaded / infinite-scroll feeds, for a small set that fits on one screen, or as a tab/section switcher (use tabs instead).
anatomy: pagination__item pagination__nav (prev arrow; add is-disabled when at the first page)
pagination__item (numbered page; add is-active for the current page)
pagination__ellipsis (static gap marker for skipped ranges)
components.interactions: Page items and arrows lift on hover (is-hover). The current page carries is-active (purple, no hover lift). is-disabled arrows are dimmed and non-interactive.

components.name: Breadcrumb
components.purpose: A compact trail showing where the current page sits in the app hierarchy — muted ancestor links joined by chevron separators, ending in a bright, non-clickable current crumb.
components.whenToUse: On detail or nested pages that live several levels deep, so the user can see the path and step back up it. Best paired with a page header on Detail-type pages.
components.whenNotToUse: On top-level sections that have no parent, on flat apps with a single navigation depth, or as a substitute for primary navigation.
anatomy: breadcrumb__item is-current (the current page — brightest, non-interactive)
components.interactions: Ancestor crumbs brighten and pick up a faint surface on hover (is-hover). The is-current crumb is non-interactive. Separators are decorative.

components.name: Stepper
components.purpose: A horizontal progress indicator for a multi-step flow — numbered markers joined by connector lines, showing which steps are done (teal check), which is current (purple ring), and which are still ahead.
components.whenToUse: On wizards, onboarding, or multi-step forms where the user should always see how many steps there are and where they stand. Advance is-active and flip finished steps to is-complete as the user progresses.
components.whenNotToUse: For a single-step form, for free navigation (use tabs), or when steps are so numerous the labels cannot fit — collapse to a simple progress bar instead.
anatomy: step is-complete — step__marker (check svg) + step__label; its connector reads teal
step is-active — step__marker (current number, purple ring) + step__label
components.interactions: Static in the reference: markers and connectors reflect flow state via is-complete / is-active / is-upcoming. Completed connectors fill teal; the active marker carries a purple focus ring.

components.name: Slider
components.purpose: A range control for picking a value along a continuum — a thin track with a blue-to-green fill up to the current value and a white, purple-ringed thumb the user drags.
components.whenToUse: When a single value is chosen from a bounded continuous range and the exact number matters less than its position — volume, opacity, a price or dosage window, playback scrubbing. Set the fill width and thumb position to the current value.
components.whenNotToUse: For precise numeric entry (use a number input), for choosing among a few discrete options (use radios or a segmented control), or when the range is unbounded.
anatomy: slider (the control; add is-disabled to dim, is-focus to pin the ring)
slider__fill (progress fill — width set inline to the current value)
slider__thumb (draggable handle — left set inline to the current value)
slider--native (alternative: a real input[type=range] skinned to match)
components.interactions: The thumb glows on hover and shows a purple focus ring on focus-within (pinned with is-focus). is-disabled dims the whole control and blocks interaction. Fill width and thumb left are the only inline values, driven by the current position.

states.label: Focus
Disabled
Native input
states.snippet: <input type="range" class="slider--native" min="0" max="100" value="70">
